notes: All poultry (breasts, whole bird, legs, thighs, wings, ground poultry, stuffing, and giblets): FSIS 165 F. No rest time. 74 C is the standard rounded conversion of 165 F (165 F = 73.9 C).
Ground beef, pork, veal, and lamb (incl. raw sausage made from ground meat): FSIS 160 F. No rest time. 71 C is the rounded conversion of 160 F (160 F = 71.1 C).

Beef, pork, veal, and lamb whole cuts (steaks, chops, roasts): FSIS 145 F PLUS a 3-minute rest before carving or eating. Fresh pork has matched beef/lamb at 145 F + 3 min since the 2011 revision. 63 C is the rounded conversion of 145 F (145 F = 62.8 C).
Finfish: FSIS 145 F (or cook until flesh is opaque and separates easily). No rest time. 63 C is the rounded conversion of 145 F.

Shrimp, lobster, crab, scallops: FSIS 145 F (flesh pearly/opaque). Clams, oysters, mussels: cook until shells open during cooking. The chart's 145 F Fish & Shellfish figure is applied to all shellfish here as the conservative single threshold.

Shell eggs: cook until both yolk and white are firm. Egg dishes (frittata, quiche, casseroles): FSIS 160 F. 71 C is the rounded conversion of 160 F. Applied to the 'egg' ingredient so raw-egg preparations (homemade mayo, mousse) hard-block in v0.
